objective: Stress-test/enhance an EXISTING business model and return a decided recommendation
Fires for a specific idea to test OR a goal wanting candidates; not zero-to-one
Wedge: diverge-by-lens -> independent per-candidate pre-mortem -> scored convergence, anchored to real economics
Produces: model snapshot -> candidate per lens -> pre-mortem -> scored table -> one recommendation (+runner-up, flip-assumptions, first-test, devil's-advocate)
inPractice: Fires on improve/enhance/stress-test an existing business model or product; quiet for zero-to-one, financial modeling, plan/deck writing, generic brainstorming
Detect evaluate-vs-generate -> tight intake -> anchor snapshot -> one candidate per lens -> independent pre-mortem each -> scored table -> one recommendation
Recommendation includes runner-up tradeoff, devil's-advocate, flip-assumptions, cheapest first-test
Guards: refuse zero-to-one, rewrite un-anchored candidates, mark lenses N/A, no invented financials, always a decision

shape.rationale: Single coherent single-output generator; no host pipeline; C pipeline-test fails. Ships one references asset.

manifest.item: diverge → stress-test → converge loop, anchored to the existing model
divergence engine forces genuinely different suggestions
every suggestion paired with concrete issues/risks
convergence rule that picks/synthesizes one recommended solution
context-intake behavior when the business model isn't supplied
manifest.note: confirmed in grill: tight intake first

manifest.item: recommendation states flip-assumptions (what would change my mind)
recommendation ends with a cheapest-validation first-test step
zero-to-one input detected and gracefully declined + redirected
non-applicable lenses marked N/A with a reason (not forced)
scored candidate table (upside·effort·risk·fit) precedes the pick
devil's-advocate pre-mortem runs on the winner before recommending

uniqueWedge: Encodes a repeatable diverge → independent-per-candidate pre-mortem → scored convergence method anchored to the user's EXISTING model's economics — forcing genuinely distinct options, an unanchored risk pass on each, and one synthesized recommendation, where a naive prompt gives near-duplicate ideas, soft critique, and a flat list.

annotations: [!OVERLAP] vs generic AI-stress-test ecosystem pattern; must encode 4 structural commitments to avoid [!GENERIC]
all manifest items authored except Verify-owned: golden replay + arena + convention check

decisions.decision: Support both generate and evaluate via a detected top-level branch
decisions.rationale: User: arrives with either a specific idea or just a goal

decisions.decision: Run a tight 3-5 question intake before diverging when model context is thin
decisions.rationale: Guarantees anchored output; skip questions already answered

decisions.decision: Converge to one recommendation plus a 'why over the runner-up' tradeoff
Force divergence via a fixed set of distinct strategic lenses (one candidate per lens)
decisions.rationale: Structurally guarantees variety; ships as a reference asset

decisions.decision: A1 flip-assumptions line on the recommendation
A4 cheapest-validation first-test on the winner
C1 scored candidate table before the pick
C4 devil's-advocate pre-mortem on the winner
